title: Cleanup and race prevention

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific React: Component-Based Browser Applications draft for Cleanup and race prevention; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: React Learn and Reference; React 19.2; provisions applicable to Cleanup and race prevention
sources.claim: An effect may return cleanup that reverses its subscription, timer, request, or connection before rerun and unmount.

canonicalExplanation.summary: An effect may return cleanup that reverses its subscription, timer, request, or connection before rerun and unmount.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A resize listener is removed with the exact same function reference when the effect cleans up. Your goal is to implement and test setup-cleanup symmetry and latest-request behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: State and events; javascript async network; Dependencies and reactive values; javascript async cancellation. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: An effect may return cleanup that reverses its subscription, timer, request, or connection before rerun and unmount. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Cleanup must correspond to the same setup, async races need abort or ignore logic, and development can setup-cleanup-setup. This draft therefore rejects: Cleanup runs only when the whole application closes, not before an effect reruns.

includes: Undo subscriptions and ignore or abort obsolete asynchronous results across rerenders and unmounts.
Implement and test setup-cleanup symmetry and latest-request behavior.
excludes: Cleanup must correspond to the same setup, async races need abort or ignore logic, and development can setup-cleanup-setup.

outcomes: Implement and test setup-cleanup symmetry and latest-request behavior.

examples.title: Cleanup and race prevention: concrete case
examples.situation: A resize listener is removed with the exact same function reference when the effect cleans up.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A resize listener is removed with the exact same function reference when the effect cleans up.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: React cleans up before a changed effect reruns and on unmount; write setup and cleanup as a reversible pair.
